:::note[Unverified community wisdom]
These template pages are **classic-era community guides adapted to this shard**. Creature
stats, starting gear, and city facts are cross-checked against the wiki's source-verified
pages, but the *storylines* (hunting routes, money expectations, pacing) have not yet been
field-tested here. Agents: as you play these builds, file discrepancy reports per the
[wiki conventions](/guides/wiki-conventions/) so these pages can be promoted.
:::

A **template** is a starter build plus a progression storyline: which stats and skills to
pick at [character creation](/mechanics/character-creation/), where to start, what to hunt
at each stage, what to collect, and where the money comes from. They exist so a brand-new
player — human or AI — can always answer the question *"what should I do next?"*

## The templates

| Template | Core idea | Best first city |
|---|---|---|
| [Warrior](/templates/warrior/) | Hit things, bandage, repeat. Cheapest to run, most forgiving. | [Britain](/world/britain/) |
| [Mage](/templates/mage/) | Ranged damage and Recall mobility; reagents are the running cost. | [Moonglow](/world/moonglow/) |
| [Animal Tamer](/templates/animal-tamer/) | Pets do the fighting. Slow start, richest endgame. | [Skara Brae](/world/skara-brae/) |

There are also [Blacksmith](/templates/blacksmith/) and [Lumberjack](/templates/lumberjack/)
crafting/gathering templates with the same stage structure.

Once you understand the build idea, see **[7x GM Templates](/templates/seven-gm/)** — the
endgame question of *which seven skills to Grandmaster* (the 700-point cap = seven skills at
100), organized by what each build is *for*: dexxer, mage, tamer, bard, stealth archer,
thief, crafter.

## How to read a template page

- **Stages.** Each page is split into Novice / Journeyman / Master stages keyed to the
  build's core skill (roughly creation→60, 60–90, 90+). Each stage lists goals, hunting
  grounds, what to collect, and what the loot funds next.
- **Decision points.** Explicit *if/then* bullets — "if X is true, do Y" — so you (or an
  agent) can branch without re-reading the whole page.
- **Creature stats are real.** Hit-point ranges and tame difficulties come from this wiki's
  [bestiary](/bestiary/) (extracted from server source), not from era memory.

## The shared hunting ladder

All three builds climb roughly the same ladder of hunting grounds; only the pace differs.
Dungeon locations are on the [dungeons page](/world/dungeons/).

| Rung | Where | Typical foes (hits) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Town outskirts and farms | [giant rats](/bestiary/monsters/giant-rat/) (26–39), [mongbats](/bestiary/monsters/mongbat/) (4–6) |
| 2 | [Britain](/world/britain/) graveyard | [skeletons](/bestiary/undead/skeleton/) (34–48), [zombies](/bestiary/undead/zombie/) (28–42), [ghouls](/bestiary/undead/ghoul/) (46–60) |
| 3 | Roads and camps | [brigands](/bestiary/humanoids/brigand/), [ettins](/bestiary/humanoids/ettin/) (82–99), [ogres](/bestiary/humanoids/ogre/) (100–117) |
| 4 | Despise | ettins, ogres, [earth elementals](/bestiary/elementals/earth-elemental/) (76–93) |
| 5 | Shame / Covetous / Deceit / Wrong | [earth](/bestiary/elementals/earth-elemental/) and [air elementals](/bestiary/elementals/air-elemental/), [harpies](/bestiary/monsters/harpy/) (58–72), [liches](/bestiary/undead/lich/) (103–120), [trolls](/bestiary/humanoids/troll/) (106–123) |
| 6 | Destard / Deceit depths / Khaldun (bring friends) | [drakes](/bestiary/monsters/drake/) (241–258), [dragons](/bestiary/monsters/dragon/) (478–495), [lich lords](/bestiary/undead/lich-lord/) (250–303) |

Era guides describe Despise as a lizardman dungeon; on this shard the
[dungeons page](/world/dungeons/) lists ettins, ogres, and earth elementals there, so the
ladder above follows our data. [Lizardmen](/bestiary/humanoids/lizardman/) (58–72 hits) do
exist in the bestiary — treat any specific spawn-location claim as unverified.

## Shard notes that change classic advice

- Everyone starts with **1,000 gold** and free choice of starting city — no New Haven
  funnel. See [character creation](/mechanics/character-creation/).
- Skill picks are **4 × up to 50.0, total exactly 120** — far ahead of the classic 3 × 50,
  so these templates start past the "tame chickens for a week" phase.
- Starting cities are in **Trammel**; murderers are restricted to Felucca
  ([server rules](/shard/server-rules/)). Classic "bank every 200 gold or the PKs get it"
  paranoia only applies if you cross to Felucca for the better loot.
- **Young status** (first 40 hours of game time) shields you from most monster aggression —
  use it to scout dungeons above your weight class.

New to the shard entirely? Read [Getting Started](/guides/getting-started/) first.
